note: seq=7 (queue): READ-ONLY production schema diagnostic for the enrichment-worker fix. The prior seq-7 worker install (since renumbered to seq-8) failed --migrate against an IMAGINED schema (column "state" does not exist; real column is status). Before re-shipping a privileged always-on worker, this fetches+sha-verifies scripts/atlas_enrich_diag.py into /opt/atlas/enrich/ and runs it: it introspects the REAL atlas.enrich_queue / atlas.field_provenance shape -- CHECK/UNIQUE/PK/FK constraints, NOT NULL, defaults, the live status value vocabulary + distribution, whether task_type is required and its values, claimable-row count under the real status model, queue<->business coverage, field_provenance source_code/field vocab, a REDACTED sample row of each table, and server_version -- and PUSHES it to status/hetzner/seq-7-diag.json via the GitHub Contents API (same channel the worker used for its self-captured migrate-error). NO DB writes, NO DDL, NO ssh/firewall/autopull touch. Read-only: the apply takes a restore point, runs one allowlisted python, passes the additive health checks, advances last_seq to 7. The corrected worker install is seq-8.

steps.note: seq=7: fetch+verify+run read-only schema diagnostic; result -> status/hetzner/seq-7-diag.json

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/enrich/atlas_enrich_diag.py':'4480488836c30e39fc4ba1a12efdfd75097913dc56e5eb9c9a6fe248f56cb053'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"
